rationale: C (with the orchestrator's C/B refinement: the store-health signal is the EXISTING MergeBase@:249 call, pinned as a named AC, not a new probe) is the only approach whose resulting spec is both minimally disrupted and fully falsifiable. Spec-coherence: R1's fail-closed asymmetry survives intact on the three genuinely-classifiable legs (epic-lookup, bd-list, ancestry via ErrRefNotFound); only the branch-existence leg reverts to a simple --quiet bool, so the ScanOrphanedClosedBeads/FindOrphanedClosedBeads wrapper-parity architecture, R3, R2, and the AC grep-discipline all stand unchanged. The revision DELETES the proven-false classifier surface (the show-ref 0/1/128 paragraphs, the GENERAL PRINCIPLE clause, TestBranchExistsE_ThreeWay/UnreadableRefStore, the impossible Falsified-if clauses) rather than patching it — the 114 delete>patch lesson. Falsifiability: the fact-5 refutation converts from prose into two pinnable, real-git-implementable ACs — (i) whole-store-unreadable (chmod 000 refs/heads) aborts ApproveImpl at MergeBase@:249 BEFORE the scan; (ii) a single-corrupt-ref orphan has no worktree, so FinalizeEpic's worktree-list loop never merges it (and a forced merge of a corrupt ref exits nonzero). The residual lands in Non-Goals with concrete evidence and a concrete falsifier — 'a reproduction that merges an orphan branch un-gated while the probe masks it and both downstream signals miss' — not a synonym-dodge. No version floor, no new probe surface, no shared-predicate divergence. Round-6 G2 stress-testing the refutation is exactly the falsification the residual invites.

rejected.A: Cleanest contract shape, but the git>=2.43 floor drags CI/install-doc scope outside 115's Impacted Domains, and the older-git fallback AC is either a hard portability regression or a re-entry into the proven-impossible classifier spiral.
rejected.B: A store-health precheck (show-ref --head / rev-parse HEAD) cannot see a single corrupt loose ref, so B duplicates MergeBase@:249's whole-store coverage while still silently depending on C's refutation for the residual — its distinguishing 'store-health-probe test' is not deterministically implementable against real git.
rejected.D: Robust by construction but demolishes the shared-predicate architecture R1/AC1/OQ2 are built on, and narrows detection to merge-imminent (worktree-bearing) orphans, contradicting the Goal's proof-of-settlement framing and next/doctor parity.
